revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天津久米命]][[zh:天津久米命]]
{{Infobox deity}}

{{基礎情報 日本の神
| 名 = Amatsukume no Mikoto
| 世代名 = 
| 先代= 
| 次代= 
| 神祇 = {{ill|Heavenly Deities|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}
| 全名 = Amatsukume no Mikoto
| 別名 = Amenokushitsu Ōkume
| 別称 = 
| 神階 = 
| 神格 = God of War
| 陵所 = 
| 父 = {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}}
| 母 = 
| 親 = 
| 配偶者 = 
| 子 = {{ill|Amatakaikane no Mikoto|ja|天多祁箇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatakaikane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| 宮 = 
| 神社 = {{ill|Ōta Shrine (Takashima)|ja|大田神社 (高島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Shrine (Takashima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kunikakari Shrine|ja|国懸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikakari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q888184}} among others
| 関連氏族 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|1=Kume clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369624}}
}}
'''Amatsukume no Mikoto''' (天津久米命) is a deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology]]. The deity is also referred to as '''Amenokushitsu Ōkume''' (天槵津大来目).
== Overview ==
Amatsukume no Mikoto is described as the {{ill|Oyagami|en|Oyagami|qid=Q85882782|lt=ancestor deity|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Amatsukume no Mikoto appears in the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} segment, and in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, the deity appears in the same scene in the fourth alternate writing. Amatsukume no Mikoto, along with {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, led {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} while armed.
In the Kojiki, Amatsukume no Mikoto and Amenooshihi no Mikoto are depicted as equals, but in the Nihon Shoki, Amenokushitsu Ōkume is shown as subordinate to Amenooshihi no Mikoto. Additionally, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|qid=Q11590444|lt=Jinmu's Eastern Expedition|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in the Nihon Shoki, the descendants of Amenokushitsu Ōkume, known as the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}, are portrayed as subordinates to {{ill|Michinookimi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinookimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}, a descendant of Amenooshihi no Mikoto. However, in the Kojiki, Michinookimi and {{ill|Ōkume no Mikoto|ja|大久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkume no Mikoto|qq=}} are depicted as equals, highlighting a similar discrepancy.
Shrines dedicated to Amatsukume no Mikoto are extremely rare.

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kume no Uta|lt=Kume no Uta|lt=Kume Songs|ja|久米歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Songs|qq=}}

{{神道 横|qq=}}
